case_title: EQBAL HUSSAIN VS. ANYUB ALI ET AL

docket_entries.description: DEFAMATION, COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF HUSSAIN, EQBAL AS TO DEFENDANT ALI, AIYUB DOES 1 TO 40 SUMMONS ISSUED, JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR JUL-18-2018 PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  APR-16-2018 CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT DUE ON         JUN-25-2018
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR (TRANSACTION ID # 100037733) FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT HUSSAIN, EQBAL

LAW AND MOTION 302,PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT EQBAL HUSSAIN'S CCP 425.16 MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANT AIYUB ALI'S CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. TO SUCCEED ON AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION, A MOVING PARTY MUST FIRST ESTABLISH A PRIMA FACIE CASE THAT THE CLAIM AROSE OUT OF AN ACT "IN FURTHERANCE OF THE PERSON'S RIGHT OF PETITION OR FREE SPEECH." (ID. AT (B)(1).) THE BURDEN THEN SHIFTS TO THE NONMOVING PARTY TO ESTABLISH A PROBABILITY THAT HE WILL PREVAIL ON HIS CLAIM. (ID.) HUSSAIN SHOWS THAT HIS E-MAIL TO ALI AND OTHER BOARD MEMBERS OF THE FIJI MUSLIM SPORTS ASSOCIATION WAS OF PUBLIC CONCERN AND THUS PROTECTED SPEECH. (SEE WILBANKS V. WOLK (2004) 121 CAL.APP.4TH 883, 897.) ALI PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT HIS CLAIM OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND DAMAGE FROM HUSSAIN'S E-MAIL. WITHOUT SUCH EVIDENCE, I CANNOT CONCLUDE THERE IS A PROBABILITY ALI WILL PREVAIL ON HIS CROSS-COMPLAINT. (SEE WILSON V. PARKER, COVERT & CHIDESTER (2002) 28 CAL.4TH 811, 821.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/RBU

Plaintiff Eqbal Hussain Motion For Attorney Fees And Expenses
Off Calendar Nov-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Eqbal Hussain Motion For Attorney Fees And Expenses.

Eqbal Hussain's motion for attorney's fees pursuant to california code of civil procedure section 425.16(c) and expenses is GRANTED. No opposition filed.

Counsel for Hussain is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Russell A. Robinson, Tel. No. 1-415-861-4416 appearing for the Plaintiff
and Cross-Defendant;
No other appearances on the matter.


RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling- Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Eqbal Hussain's CCP 425.16 motion to strike defendant Aiyub Ali's cross-complaint is GRANTED. To succeed on an anti-SLAPP motion, a moving party must first establish a prima facie case that the claim arose out of an act "in furtherance of the person's right of petition or free speech." (Id. at (b)(1).) The burden then shifts to the nonmoving party to establish a probability that he will prevail on his claim. (Id.) Hussain shows that his e-mail to Ali and other board members of the Fiji Muslim Sports Association was of public concern and thus protected speech. (See Wilbanks v. Wolk (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 883, 897.) Ali presents no evidence to support his claim of emotional distress and damage from Hussain's e-mail. Without such evidence, I cannot conclude there is a probability Ali will prevail on his cross-complaint. (See Wilson v. Parker, Covert & Chidester (2002) 28 Cal.4th 811, 821.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/rbu
